festivals.name: Esala Perahera (Kandy)
festivals.description: One of Asia's grandest processions: 50 decorated elephants and thousands of dancers carry the Sacred Tooth Relic through Kandy.

festivals.name: Sinhala & Tamil New Year
festivals.description: The Sinhala and Tamil New Year is celebrated with traditional games, oil lamp rituals, and the first meal of the new year.

destinations.name: Sigiriya
destinations.description: A 5th-century palace and fortress atop a 180m granite pinnacle with ancient frescoes and water gardens.

destinations.name: Kandy
destinations.description: The last royal capital has the Sacred Tooth Relic temple, surrounded by tea estates and the scenic Mahaweli River.

destinations.name: Colombo
destinations.description: Sri Lanka's capital mixes Dutch colonial forts and Buddhist temples with a booming food and arts scene.

destinations.name: Yala National Park
destinations.description: Sri Lanka's most visited park has the world's highest density of leopards alongside elephants and coastal bird life.

destinations.name: Ella
destinations.description: A misty hill station with the iconic Nine Arch colonial viaduct, tea pluckers in green fields, and the hike to Little Adam's Peak.

destinations.name: Galle
destinations.description: A UNESCO Dutch colonial fort on the southern tip of Sri Lanka with galleries, boutique hotels, and surf beaches.

destinations.name: Anuradhapura
destinations.description: Sri Lanka's first ancient capital has massive white-washed dagobas and the world's oldest documented living tree.

destinations.name: Mirissa (Whale Watching)
destinations.description: The world's best place to see blue whales — the largest animals to have ever lived — in their natural habitat offshore.
